name: Leningrad Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the upper field bearing the coat of arms; also a wavy stripe
the lower wavy stripe; the field of the central shield with anchor and key
the upper wavy stripe; the upper portion of the shield with embattled wall
the golden key crossed with the anchor

description: A white field occupying the upper two-thirds of the flag, with the oblast coat of arms (anchor and key crossed on blue, embattled wall on red) in the centre; below, three wavy stripes - red, white, and blue - run along the bottom.
history: Adopted by the Leningrad Oblast Legislative Assembly on 9 December 1997.

features.symbolism: white field over wavy red, white, and blue stripes; symbolises the region as the maritime gateway of Russia, with the wavy white-blue-red tricolour echoing the Russian national flag and the loyalty of the region to Russia.
silver anchor crossed with a golden key on a blue field, beneath a silver embattled wall on red - symbolising the region as the maritime and continental gateway of Russia.
